Guardian's Dagger  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 30
DMG: 8 Dmg Bonus: 35
Backstab DMG: 8
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
DEX: +2 STA: +3 AGI: +3
WT: 0.5 Size: TINY
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG BST
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

8 damage ?
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2001 at 8:19 AM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
Hm... i got this weapon yesterday on a trade and have looked at it since then and it says it is an 9/30 weapon... not 8/30. Anybody else seen this discrepency or am I just going crazy????

Still dropping on Vaezelle
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2001 at 11:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246807636
14 posts
Befallen hasn't returned to its original state on Vaezelle, if it ever will. Picked one of these up off a Guardian of T'Vyl just this evening.
▲ Page top
WOW!!!!
QuoteReply
# Aug 17 2001 at 7:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248242941
1 post
I have to say this dagger is sweet. I found it on a vendor in EC last night for a little over 4pp. WOW I started grouping and was out damaging the warriors in my group who were as much as 4 levels higher than me.

Granted at the time I was only 5th level rogue, but i forsee using this little baby for a long time to come.

I Was offered this for only 60pp CRAP CRAP CRAP. But Rejected. Fainting, Fainting <Falls into pool of CRAP> CCRRAAPP!!
▲ Page top
RE: CRAP
QuoteReply
# Aug 05 2001 at 8:40 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756368265
66 posts
I hate to flame, but a 12th lvl rogue calling an 8 damage weapon crap screams "twink" to me. I didn't get an 8 damage weapon till I managed to save up and buy a serated bone dirk around lvl 16, and most of the money I had to borrow from a lvl 50 guildie. Granted, at this point in my roguing career, I wouldn't want this, I would have loved it for 60 plat at level 12, when I was using a dragoon dirk.

Saldiven Wurmfood
Rogue of 50 Heists
Cazic-Thule
▲ Page top
RE: CRAP
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2001 at 2:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767829862
Guru
316 posts
At the risk of pointing out what I thought was obvious, I believe the reference to "Crap" was his frustration and disappointment in himself for not picking up the weapon when it was offered at a nice price.
▲ Page top
RE: CRAP
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2001 at 2:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249542308
5 posts
I have to say I am a twinked out rouge character weilding a blood stained fang and i still think this weapon is nice. I do not think I would have paid 60pp at 12 maybe more like 25pp but it is still a nice weapon.

Oh I twinked him with stuff not money he had to make that on his own. If I had 60pp at 12 it would be mainly spent on bandaids and food and water.

Have you ever noticed that you drink alot of water in Nro? =)

If Befallen originally had DEs all over the frickin place and I mean ALL over it...then it has not changed back on Prexus Server. I was there yesterday...actually I died there yesterday. Hehe, Befallen got changed back yesterday.

I went down the well with 29 and 25 druids and a 28 enchanter. Heavy fighting at the beginning back the spawn was slow so it got pretty comortable.

Picked one of these babies up on a pull and now am trying to sell.

Nice stats, was going to give to my rogue, but hes got a Stiletto of the Bloodclaw....

Actually, I have both SotB and Guardian Dagger (after a kind Ogre SK gave it to me for services rendered). I use the GD for backstabs - SotB yields 44 bs, whereas GD yields 61.

▲ Page top
Drops off
QuoteReply
# Jul 01 2001 at 11:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755202738
25 posts
Guardians of T`Vyl round level 25, very common drop on the 3rd floor. Look out for the backstab on these SOB's... Hittin me at level 22 for 170+ damage BSing! And the spawn rate on the 3rd floor is insane. 10 minute spawns and there are mobs everywhere! Very easy to get surrounded. Total madhouse. I wouldn't suggest soloing it at any level. =)
▲ Page top
RE: Drops off
QuoteReply
# Jul 07 2001 at 2:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757142046
37 posts
Very easy to solo - just don't run around like a mad hatter. And I am yet to be backstabbed by any Guardians or Servants... Guess it's cos I don't turn my back to em... Good philosophy that.
▲ Page top
RE: Drops off
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2001 at 5:50 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Not really, unless you are very lucky.

Tried it with a group of 30ies and saw the hugest train in my life. Worse than the "whole house" trains in Unrest or the famous BlackBorrow trains.
Stupid things can see trough walls and are agressive, even when green. So you think you pull 1 greenie and kaboom - 15-20 MoB train whacking you down.

You could solo at the entrance door to lvl 3 or down the well, but unless you want a hell of CR I wouldn't recommend it - and better have a bard or chanter for crowd control AND a Druid with evac in your group.
▲ Page top
RE: Drops off
QuoteReply
# Jul 27 2001 at 4:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762996353
119 posts
Actually, there is a place where you can pull Guardians 'til the cows come home and possibly never suffer a huge train. Go through the secret wall midway through the main hall on the third level, past the Cmdr Windstream spawn area, and then into the main cave room, then turn right. You go down a short, somwhat curved hallway, and you will see a dark elf female standing against the wall. In the little hallway leading here, there are two Guardian spawn points. Simply wait here in this room, and when the Guardian(s) pop, pull them into the room. The dark elf female will cast a few spells on them, actually assisting you, and you can proceed to beat the snot out of em. Just put some sort of snare or root effect on em and you will be fine. However, do not sit too close to the walls here while you med, for while there are no pops in the room itself, I have had a mob aggro me through the wall, training the entire second level on me. It was ugly.
▲ Page top
